action.reasoning: Observation: Training failed with RDKit RuntimeError 'bond already exists' during MolDataset init for train (after loading data, during FP computation on ~350k mols). No eval score yet after 5 iters; PyG imports segfaulted previously. Analysis: OGB mol graphs are undirected so edge_index lists both (u,v) and (v,u) for each bond, causing duplicate AddBond calls on RWMol. FP computation also slow without caching (will dominate first iter). Decision: Fix get_morgan_fingerprint to add bonds only if start < end, add optional caching of fps tensors to disk (train_fps.pt) for fast future iters, keep full data + 3 epochs, rerun train then submit_eval for first avg_precision baseline and time measurement.
